description: Empirical daily return distributions for S&P 500 and Bitcoin, used for pricing bracket markets on Kalshi

sp500.note: VIX/16 gives daily 1-sigma expected move. VIX overestimates actual vol ~60% of time (volatility risk premium). Markets priced at VIX-implied levels tend to overpay for insurance.

bitcoin.note: DVOL/sqrt(365) gives daily expected move. BTC has fat tails — use Cauchy or Student-t distribution, not normal. Regime-switching: calm periods have much lower vol than average.

strategy_implication: For S&P brackets: short-volatility bias works most of the time (VIX overestimates). For BTC: wider brackets are safer due to fat tails. Both: condition on current VIX/DVOL level, not unconditional distributions.
